reviews.review_description:
This demo app is unreal for the little that is shown in the demo.  Can you post an update where you are at in the development of the finished product?  There seems to be little information and this app seems to have some extreme potential,  I loved how the ceiling opens up and then you can lower the walls.  Maybe partner with Griffith Observatory or some other planetarium  and get a finished product out there.  Give an update please to let us all know if the demo is all we are going to get because
The project Is dead or if you are still working on it.  Cheers

reviews.review_title: I would pay for a finished app
reviews.review_description: Please develop this to a finished app

reviews.review_title: It's what I've been waiting for...
reviews.review_description: But it could use some tweaking.   I would allow for a person to look under themselves as well,  not just the upper half of the room.   I want to see Leo, but it's currently not in my viewing area and that blows.  I loved the Planetarium and I can't wait to see the full version!! This is a win for me.  Let's get the full version going!!
I want to unlock the geolocation ..is it not done?

reviews.review_title: Best VR Planetarium in the world!
reviews.review_description: Congrats to the developer! Anxiously Waiting for the final app!!
Can't wait to show this to my dad the next time I visit. He loves space stuff and has been blown away by other VR space experiences. Looking forward to his reaction when the ceiling opens.
Nice use of AR here, really good demo to show people. My VR room has 5 walls though, so more than 4 points of tracking would be good.
AR is gonna be huge. This right here proves it.

reviews.review_title: Not the noat intricate pass through demo
reviews.review_description: But very well presented and pleasant. Good job.

the bones for an absolutely amazing app are all there. My suggestion is upgrade the textures for quest 3 as thats where an app like this can really shine. give us the full resolution of the displays and take advange of the sharpness to really give the stars some detail and I would sit in there for hours. as it sits right now is just too blury especially watching the planetarium stuff. Cant wait for the full release!

I can only echo what the other reviewers have said about this app.
The use of AR is something that excites me in VR and this app demonstrates how fantastic it is, and in time how the gaming/app world is going to change.
Altho of course it would never beat standing in the freezing cold with a cup of *Bovril, staring at the mysteries that the skies have to offer.
* other meaty drinks are available 

Well done Dev's 
A fantastic showing
I know it isnt finished yet but I love where this is going! Very very cool
It brings us wealth of knowledge about the starry sky and makes us feel as if we are on the scene.

This is a great app. I enjoy looking at the night sky from within my home. It’s pretty cool.
One suggested though.
It would be cool to look at different skies from different planets. Instead of making that too complicated, it would just be a day and night sky for all the planets in the solar system. Including things like a sunset on mars. Or an earthrise on the moon. That would be a cool feature
Love it so much! I really hope you're able to release the full version soon. It's such a cool effect to see my ceiling open up to the sky and the walls drop. The planetarium looks amazing.

It's a very good tool, but I would like to suggest improvements.
・The sky is too bright. It would be nice to be able to adjust the darkness of the sky.
・There is no need for daytime expressions. It would be nice to be able to turn on/off the dayltime.
・It would be nice to be able to advance or return the current time with the stick.
・It would be nice if there was a mode where there is no text display at all.
How do I get the full cersion? Satellite,  compass etc

what a unique AR experience it felt like my roof did open exposing the stars above as I look at my dog sitting on my black and white pass-through couch below, I could only imagine what this will look like in color pass-through in the future.  My only complaint is my neck got sore from looking up after a while going to set up my reclining computer chain in the middle of my room for my next watch.
Great job!

when i first got my oculus, all i wanted was an app that’ll give me an experience of seeing stars the way i used to see them, living on a boat, in the middle of the Pacific.  i waited almost a year before something promising.

months ago, i saw previews of this app on reddit and knew there wasnt going to be anything that could compare.

today it was released and it is everything i wanted and more.  

this is an amazingly beautiful piece of art that will be worth every penny that it sells for. 

thank you for sharing this with us.
